rulings.Case Title: ALAN STEUER ET AL VS. CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 1. PLAINTIFFS ALAN STEUER IN HIS CAPACITY AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE SYUFY TRUST, AND STAFFORD SMILEY's Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Plaintiffs' "motion for entry of judgment" is deferred to Judge East, who granted plaintiffs' summary adjudication motion on November 12, 2024. The parties dispute whether Judge East intended to completely dispose of the action or not. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BIKOFF VS. LIONTREE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 1. DEFENDANT's Motion For A Protective Order. Defendants' motion for a discovery protective order is denied. "Defendants move for a protective order against all of Plaintiff's interrogatories and RFPs propounded on October 2, 2024, and also the topics and RFPs contained in the PMK Deposition Notice served on October 4." (Not. 2:9-12.) This is overbroad. Without a particularized showing regarding individual discovery requests and deposition topics, the court cannot know whether all are improper. Defendants provide only "a limited non-exhaustive set of examples." (Memo. 5:1-2; see also 6:22.) For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion/Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =302/RBU

Defendants' demurrer, which is limited to the complaint's third count (intentional misrepresentation), is overruled. Count 3 is adequately alleged for the pleading stage. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ZAHIR NASERI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 9. PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI's MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT. Denied as untimely filed. (CCP 1005(b).) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FORTIS ADVISORS LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. STRATASYS LTD., AN ISRAELI CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Seal Portions Of The Complaint And Certain Exhibits Thereto
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Tuesday, December 17, 2024, Line 8. DEFENDANT STRATASYS, INC.'s Motion To Seal Portions Of The Complaint And Certain Exhibits Thereto. Stratasys, Inc.'s unopposed motion to seal portions of complaint and certain exhibits thereto is granted. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and complies with the findings requirements of CRC 2.550 and specifically lists the sealed information and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.
